trade.summary: Spain and Sweden have solid bilateral trade. Spain exports food products, vehicles, and chemicals; Sweden exports vehicles, machinery, pharmaceuticals, and telecommunications equipment. Both benefit from EU single market. IKEA, H&M, and Volvo have major Spanish operations.

diplomatic.summary: Spain and Sweden maintain excellent diplomatic relations as EU members and now both NATO members (Sweden joined 2024). Both are democracies cooperating on Ukraine, climate, and digital governance. Different positions on some EU fiscal matters (Sweden more fiscally conservative) but broadly aligned on EU policy. Both strongly support Ukraine.

military.summary: Both NATO members since 2024. Spain supported Sweden's NATO accession. Joint exercises within NATO frameworks. Both operate Gripen and Eurofighter aircraft respectively in NATO air policing. Spain has NATO responsibilities in the South; Sweden in the North. Complementary roles.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Swedish firms (IKEA, H&M, Volvo, Ericsson, Spotify) have major Spanish operations. Spanish firms (Inditex, Mango) compete in Swedish retail market. Both share EU regulatory environment though Sweden retains its own currency (krona).

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Sweden is a major renewable electricity producer (hydro, nuclear, wind). Spain is a major renewable electricity producer (solar, wind) and LNG hub. Both cooperate on EU energy transition. Potential for indirect energy complementarity as both become renewable exporters.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. Spanish and Swedish universities partner extensively in life sciences, climate, physics, and environmental sciences. Erasmus exchanges are significant. Karolinska and Swedish universities collaborate with Spanish counterparts in medical research.
